axioms.name: na_anugamyante_paryaya
axioms.cite: the sistas do not so treat them: na anugamyante paryaya iva

axioms.name: samskara_sadhita
axioms.cite: gavi is *corrected to* gauh, never the reverse

axioms.name: pratipannas
axioms.cite: the asymmetry is codified smrti-sastrena, by the received science

axioms.name: asaksat_vackaka
axioms.cite: the corrupt forms are not *directly* expressive

denials.name: paryaya_bhava
denials.cite: in cultivated usage the pair never behaves as a synonym-pair does

denials.name: vackaka
denials.cite: the corrupt forms are not *directly* expressive

rejected_readings.label: synonym_equivalence
rejected_readings.rendering: Corrupt and correct forms are complete synonyms used interchangeably by cultivated speakers.
rejected_readings.why: Contradicts the core finding that cultivated speakers treat them asymmetrically (gavi corrected to gauh, never reverse). The commentary states in cultivated usage they never behave as a synonym-pair. This erases the directional correction that grounds the semantic consequence.

rejected_readings.label: direct_expressiveness
rejected_readings.rendering: Corrupt forms directly express meaning with the same immediacy as correct forms.
rejected_readings.why: The commentary explicitly states the corrupt forms are not *directly* expressive; their conveying is mediated and inferential (1.149). This reading contradicts the semantic thesis the verse establishes.

notes: The contract formalizes the verse's argument from sociological datum (corrective asymmetry: gavi→gauh, not vice versa) to semantic thesis (corrupt forms lack direct expressiveness). smṛti-śāstra certifies the asymmetry, grounding theory in the practice of śiṣṭas (1.27-31, 1.137). The contested reading (smṛti as cause vs. certifier) is resolved toward the epistemology where practice is deep evidence and theory follows it.
